channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump talks Gaza takeover and other takeaways from his appearance with Netanyahu
item.description: President Donald Trump offered a jaw-dropping performance during his joint news conference with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, suggesting that Palestinian refugees from the Gaza Strip could be permanently resettled elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Michelle L. Price And Zeke Miller, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge blocks transfers of 3 transgender inmates to men's prison
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge agreed Tuesday to temporarily block prison officials from transferring three incarcerated transgender women to men’s facilities and terminating their access to hormone therapy under an executive order signed by President Donald Trump. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Pam Bondi as US attorney general, putting Trump ally at Justice Department’s helm
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Senate confirmed Pam Bondi as U.S. attorney general Tuesday evening, putting a longtime ally of Donald Trump at the helm of a Justice Department that has already been rattled by the firings of career employees seen as disloyal to the Republican president.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Pam Bondi as US attorney general
item.description: Pam Bondi's confirmation as U.S. attorney general comes at a time when the Justice Department and FBI are under heavy scrutiny by Democrats and Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Altered image of Wisconsin Supreme Court candidate in new ad raises ethics concerns
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — A new television attack ad in Wisconsin's hotly contested Supreme Court race features a doctored image of the liberal candidate, a move that her campaign claims could be a violation of a recently enacted state law.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill count swells to 484 as Nevada Legislature hits Day 2; Voter ID a Republican goal
item.description: Packed binders. Busy halls. Lobbyists, lawmakers and journalists roaming amongst each other. This is when the routine of the state government sets in. Already, there are 484 bills submitted this session, with many more expected.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he wants the U.S. to take ownership of the Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump and Israeli leader Benjamin Netanyahu met Tuesday at the White House where Trump floated the idea of the U.S. taking ownership of the Gaza Strip and redeveloping the territory.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr. pass committee hurdles in win for Trump
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Tuesday cleared crucial hurdles toward winning Senate confirmation, handing President Trump a key win as he looks for an early display of loyalty from GOP lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Congress puts hold on Trump's $1 billion arms sale to Israel
item.description: Two congressional aides told The Hill on Tuesday that a hold has been placed on the arms sale package.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, NewsNation

item.title: Trump warns he left instructions to ‘obliterate’ Iran if he is assassinated
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday said that he has left instructions that if Iran were to assassination him, the country should be destroyed.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump doubles down on floating Canada as 51st state amid tariff dispute
item.description: President Trump on Monday reiterated his desire to see Canada become part of the United States as he prepared to impose steep tariffs on Canadian goods.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Church vandalized by Proud Boys now controls group's trademark, judge rules
item.description: The Proud Boys is barred from selling merchandise using its name or symbols without permission from a Black church once vandalized by the right-wing extremist group's members, after a Washington, D.C., judge on Monday revoked its control over the trademark.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Trump oil tariffs expected to raise price at the pump
item.description: President Trump’s tariff on Canadian oil is expected to raise prices at the pump for U.S. consumers, if it takes effect as planned after a 30-day delay.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Purges at FBI, DOJ trigger 'battle' for career staff
item.description: The Trump administration has removed dozens of Department of Justice (DOJ) and FBI officials and is eyeing firing perhaps thousands more in an unprecedented purge that may just be getting started.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: 5 things to know about Trump's plan to create a sovereign wealth fund
item.description: President Donald Trump is taking steps to overhaul the way the U.S. government invests its money and change the nature of America’s financial presence abroad.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, Fox 5

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr. face make-or-break moment after perilous hearings
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. are staring down crucial tests on Tuesday as Senate committees will decide whether to advance their nominations to the floor amid increasing pressure from allies of President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Schools poised to be major front line in Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: Oklahoma is moving to require proof of citizenship for the guardians of public school students, while California schools are distributing "know your rights" cards to immigrant families as K-12 campuses become a crucial front line in President Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on Pam Bondi AG nomination
item.description: The Senate on Monday teed up a final vote on Pam Bondi's nomination to lead the Justice Department. The chamber voted 52-46 to limit debate. Absent an agreement with Democrats, that puts a final confirmation vote on the docket for the early hours of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Zilbermints, NewsNation

item.title: GOP's Susan Collins to back Tulsi Gabbard confirmation
item.description: Sen. Susan Collins (R-Maine) announced Monday that she will support Tulsi Gabbard to become director of national intelligence (DNI), giving her a key boost in her confirmation push.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: NYC mayor downplays economic concerns of Trump deportation plan
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- There have anecdotal reports that immigration raids are driving undocumented New Yorkers into the shadows. New York City has more than 400,000 undocumented workers who help prop up the economy; however, Mayor Eric Adams said he was not worried they would stop working.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Elon Musk-led DOGE effectively shuts down USAID
item.description: Democratic lawmakers say shutting down the U.S. Agency for International Development is illegal, vowing legal challenges.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Who gets more disaster aid? Republican states. Here's the breakdown
item.description: Experts say every state gets millions in federal help — and a little more goes to Republican states and congressional districts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
